item.text: I’m editing `prd.json` now to add the runtime model registry/defaults and seed the `mvp_cli_control` phase with an EPAM provider + phase default model. I’m also tagging the pending stories in that phase so the new resolver has both a phase-default case and a story-override case to exercise.

I’m replacing the old `effort => model` behavior in `claude.sh` with a resolver that keeps effort only for turn budgets. Model now comes from metadata precedence, with an explicit legacy fallback only for non-EPAM providers so the older health-check stories don’t break.

The next edits wire the resolver through the execution path: prompt context, dry-run visibility, pre-phase validation, monitor/event payloads, and phase-cost logging. After that I’ll add the hidden self-test harness and run it against temp fixtures.

item.aggregated_output:
local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)
    local epoch
    epoch=$(date +%s)
    local msg_id="status_${story_id}_${epoch}"

    local message
    message=$(jq -cn \
        --arg id      "$msg_id" \
        --arg ts      "$timestamp" \
        --arg from    "$agent_role" \
        --arg to      "orchestrator" \
        --arg sid     "$story_id" \
        --arg pid     "$phase_id" \
        --arg subj    "Story $story_id $status" \
        --arg body    "Story $story_id finished with status: $status" \
        --arg sstatus "$status" \
        '{
            id: $id, timestamp: $ts,
            from_agent: $from, to_agent: $to,
            story_id: $sid, phase_id: $pid,
            message_type: "status",
            priority: "normal",
            subject: $subj, body: $body,
            story_status: $sstatus,
            status: "new"
        }')

    touch "$messages_jsonl"
    (
        flock -w 10 200 || return 0
        echo "$message" | jq -c '.' >> "$messages_jsonl"
    ) 200>"$lock_file"
}

# Log event to agent-status.json if running in orchestration mode
log_to_monitor() {
    local event_type=$1
    local story_id=$2
    local message=$3
    local monitor_file="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs/agent-status.json"

    # Only log if monitor file exists (orchestration mode)
    if [ ! -f "$monitor_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local lane="${WORKTREE_MODE:-main}"
    local role=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // ""' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    local timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)

    # Use flock to prevent race conditions
    (
        flock -w 5 200 || return 1

        # Add event to events array
        local tmp_file="${monitor_file}.tmp.$$"
        jq --arg type "$event_type" \
           --arg story "$story_id" \
           --arg lane "$lane" \
           --arg role "$role" \
           --arg msg "$message" \
           --arg ts "$timestamp" \
           '.events += [{"type": $type, "story": $story, "lane": $lane, "role": $role, "message": $msg, "timestamp": $ts}]' \
           "$monitor_file" > "$tmp_file" && mv "$tmp_file" "$monitor_file"
    ) 200>"${monitor_file}.lock"
}

# Get list of available phases
get_phases() {
    jq -r '.implementationOrder | keys[]' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null
}

# Get stories for a specific phase
get_phase_stories() {
    local phase=$1
    jq -r --arg phase "$phase" '.implementationOrder[$phase] // [] | .[]' "$PRD_FILE"
}

# Get the phase a story belongs to
get_story_phase() {
    local story_id=$1
    jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.implementationOrder | to_entries[] | select(.value | contains([$id])) | .key' "$PRD_FILE" | head -1
}

# Get list of incomplete stories
get_incomplete_stories() {
    jq -r '.stories[] | select(.completed == false) | .id' "$PRD_FILE"
}

# Get prioritized list of incomplete stories (respects phases, dependencies, priority)
get_prioritized_stories() {
    local result=()

    # Get phases in order
    local phases=$(get_phases)

    if [ -z "$phases" ]; then
        # No phases defined, fall back to all incomplete stories sorted by priority
        jq -r '.stories[] | select(.completed == false) | "\(.priority // "medium")|\(.id)"' "$PRD_FILE" | \
            sort -t'|' -k1,1 | cut -d'|' -f2
        return
    fi

    # Process each phase in order
    while IFS= read -r phase; do
        [ -z "$phase" ] && continue

        # Get stories in this phase
        local phase_stories=$(get_phase_stories "$phase")

        # For each story in the phase, check if it's incomplete and dependencies are met
        while IFS= read -r story_id; do
            [ -z "$story_id" ] && continue

            if ! is_story_completed "$story_id" && are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
                echo "$story_id"
            fi
        done <<< "$phase_stories"
    done <<< "$phases"
}

# Get next story to implement (first incomplete with satisfied dependencies)
get_next_story() {
    get_prioritized_stories | head -1
}

# List available phases with status
list_phases() {
    echo ""
    echo -e "${MAGENTA}=== Implementation Phases ===${NC}"
    echo ""

    local phases=$(get_phases)

    if [ -z "$phases" ]; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}No phases defined in implementationOrder${NC}"
        return
    fi

    while IFS= read -r phase; do
        [ -z "$phase" ] && continue

        local total=0
        local completed=0
        local stories=$(get_phase_stories "$phase")

        while IFS= read -r story_id; do
            [ -z "$story_id" ] && continue
            total=$((total + 1))
            if is_story_completed "$story_id"; then
                completed=$((completed + 1))
            fi
        done <<< "$stories"

        local status_color=$YELLOW
        local status_icon="o"
        if [ $completed -eq $total ] && [ $total -gt 0 ]; then
            status_color=$GREEN
            status_icon="+"
        elif [ $completed -gt 0 ]; then
            status_color=$CYAN
            status_icon="~"
        fi

        echo -e "${status_color}${status_icon}${NC} ${WHITE}$phase${NC} ($completed/$total completed)"

        # Show stories in phase
        while IFS= read -r story_id; do
            [ -z "$story_id" ] && continue
            local title=$(get_story_title "$story_id")
            if is_story_completed "$story_id"; then
                echo -e "    ${GREEN}+${NC} $story_id: $title"
            else
                local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id" | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
                local deps_info=""
                if [ -n "$deps" ]; then
                    if are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
                        deps_info=" ${CYAN}(deps: $deps)${NC}"
                    else
                        deps_info=" ${RED}(blocked by: $deps)${NC}"

echo "$prompt" >> "$output_file"
        echo "=== End Prompt ===" >> "$output_file"
        echo "" >> "$output_file"

        log "Invoking $story_cli (attempt $((retry_count + 1))/$((MAX_RETRIES + 1)))..."

        # Change to project root for the CLI to have correct context
        cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"

        echo "=== $story_cli Output (attempt $((retry_count + 1))) ===" >> "$output_file"

        local json_result_file="${output_file%.log}_result.json"
        local invoke_success=false

        case "${STORY_PROVIDER:-claude-sonnet}" in
            opencode)
                # OpenCode: pass prompt via temp file (prompts can exceed arg limits)
                # --format json emits JSONL stream; we normalize it after
                local raw_file="${json_result_file%.json}_raw.jsonl"
                local prompt_file="${json_result_file%.json}_prompt.txt"
                echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"
                if opencode run --format json "$(cat "$prompt_file")" \
                        > "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null; then
                    normalize_provider_json "opencode" "$raw_file" "$json_result_file"
                    # Append text output to log
                    grep '"type":"text"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
                        | jq -r '.part.text // .text // empty' 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || true
                    invoke_success=true
                fi
                rm -f "$prompt_file"
                ;;
            codex)
                # Codex: reads prompt from stdin when '-' is passed
                # --json emits JSONL stream; we normalize it after
                local raw_file="${json_result_file%.json}_raw.jsonl"
                if echo "$prompt" | codex exec --json - \
                        > "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null; then
                    normalize_provider_json "codex" "$raw_file" "$json_result_file"
                    # Append text output to log
                    grep '"type":"item.completed"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
                        | jq -r '.item.text // empty' 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || true
                    invoke_success=true
                fi
                ;;
            codemie-claude)
                # codemie-claude: same invocation pattern as claude — --print --output-format json
                if echo "$prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE codemie-claude --print --output-format json \
                        "${model_flag[@]}" "${turns_flag[@]}" "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" \
                        2>/dev/null > "$json_result_file"; then
                    invoke_success=true
                fi
                ;;
            epam)
                # epam: treat same as claude — same CLI, same output format
                if echo "$prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --print --output-format json \
                        "${model_flag[@]}" "${turns_flag[@]}" "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" \
                        2>/dev/null > "$json_result_file"; then
                    invoke_success=true
                fi
                ;;
            *)
                # Claude (claude-sonnet, claude-opus, or any claude-* value)
                # --print --output-format json captures cost+tokens in a single JSON object
                if echo "$prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --print --output-format json \
                        "${model_flag[@]}" "${turns_flag[@]}" "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" \
                        2>/dev/null > "$json_result_file"; then
                    invoke_success=true
                fi
                ;;
        esac

        if [ "$invoke_success" = true ]; then
            # Extract human-readable result text and append to output log
            if [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
                jq -r '.result // empty' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || cat "$json_result_file" >> "$output_file"
            fi
            echo "" >> "$output_file"
            echo "=== End $story_cli Output ===" >> "$output_file"
            success "$story_cli completed implementation for $story_id"
            update_monitor_status "complete" "$story_id" "Implementation succeeded"
            append_cost_record "$story_id" "completed" "$story_started_at" "$(date -Iseconds)" "$output_file" "$json_result_file"
            post_completion_message "$story_id" "completed"
            return 0
        else
            local exit_code=$?
            # Still capture any partial JSON output
            if [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
                jq -r '.result // empty' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || cat "$json_result_file" >> "$output_file"
            fi
            echo "" >> "$output_file"
            echo "=== $story_cli exited with code $exit_code ===" >> "$output_file"

            retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
            if [ $retry_count -le $MAX_RETRIES ]; then
                warning "$story_cli failed, retrying in ${RETRY_DELAY}s..."
                sleep $RETRY_DELAY
            fi
        fi
    done

    error "Failed to implement $story_id after $((MAX_RETRIES + 1)) attempts"
    update_monitor_status "fail" "$story_id" "Failed after $((MAX_RETRIES + 1)) attempts"
    append_cost_record "$story_id" "failed" "$story_started_at" "$(date -Iseconds)" "$output_file" "$json_result_file"
    post_completion_message "$story_id" "failed"
    return 1
}

# Update story status in PRD
update_story_status() {
    local story_id=$1
    local status=$2  # "completed" or "failed"
    local timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)
    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
    local lock_file="${prd_target}.lock"

    local tmp_file="${prd_target}.tmp.$$"

Global Acceptance Criteria:
- $criteria
EOF
}

# Build prompt for Claude to implement a story
build_implementation_prompt() {
    local story_id=$1
    local story_json=$(get_story_details "$story_id")
    local story_type=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.storyType // "implementation"')
    local provider=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.aiProvider // "claude-sonnet"')

    # Health checks and infra smoke tests should stay minimal and deterministic.
    # These stories validate orchestration plumbing, not feature implementation.
    if [ "$story_type" = "health_check" ]; then
        cat << EOF
Run a single-turn infrastructure smoke test for $story_id.

Provider context:
- aiProvider: $provider

Instructions:
1. Reply with exactly this lowercase word and nothing else: hello
2. Do not call any tools
3. Do not modify any files
4. Do not include markdown fences
EOF
        return
    fi

    local title=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.title')
    local description=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.description')
    local acceptance_criteria=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.acceptanceCriteria | join("\n- ")')
    local technical_notes=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.technicalNotes // empty')
    local files=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.technicalNotes.files // [] | join(", ")')
    local file_count=$(echo "$story_json" | jq '[.technicalNotes.files // [] | .[]] | length')
    local dependencies=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.dependencies | join(", ")')
    local required_skills=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.technicalNotes.requiredSkills // [] | join(", ")')

    # ── Rule 1: Language mismatch detection ──────────────────────────────
    # Compare story's requiredSkills against the project's primary language.
    # If the story targets a different language (e.g. Python in a TypeScript
    # project), suppress "follow existing patterns" to prevent the agent from
    # exploring irrelevant source code.
    local project_lang
    project_lang=$(jq -r '.project.stack.language // "" | ascii_downcase' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    local lang_match=true
    if [ -n "$project_lang" ] && [ -n "$required_skills" ]; then
        if ! echo "$required_skills" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | grep -qiw "$project_lang"; then
            lang_match=false
        fi
    fi

    local pattern_instruction scope_instruction
    if [ "$lang_match" = false ]; then
        pattern_instruction="This task uses a different language/stack than the main project. Do NOT explore the broader codebase for patterns — implement using standard practices for the task's own language."
    else
        pattern_instruction="Follow the project's existing code patterns and conventions."
    fi

    # ── Rule 2: Scope boundary from file list ────────────────────────────
    # When technicalNotes.files is present, constrain the agent to those
    # paths to prevent unnecessary context loading.
    if [ "$file_count" -gt 0 ]; then
        scope_instruction="Your scope is limited to these files: ${files}. Do not read or explore files outside this list unless a listed file explicitly imports from them."
    else
        scope_instruction=""
    fi

    cat << EOF
Implement user story $story_id: $title

## Story Description
$description

## Acceptance Criteria
- $acceptance_criteria

## Technical Notes
$([ -n "$technical_notes" ] && echo "$technical_notes" | jq -r 'to_entries | map("- \(.key): \(.value)") | join("\n")' 2>/dev/null || echo "None specified")

## Files to Create/Modify
$files

## Dependencies
${dependencies:-None}

## Instructions
1. Implement all acceptance criteria for this story
2. ${pattern_instruction}
3. Create any necessary files in the locations specified
4. Ensure code compiles/runs without errors
5. Do NOT create tests unless explicitly required in acceptance criteria
$([ -n "$scope_instruction" ] && echo "6. ${scope_instruction}")

After implementation, provide a brief summary of what was created/modified.
EOF
}

# Update monitor lane/story status via update-monitor.sh
update_monitor_status() {
    local event="$1"   # "start" | "complete" | "fail"
    local story_id="$2"
    local message="${3:-}"
    local lane="${WORKTREE_MODE:-main}"
    local title
    title=$(get_story_title "$story_id" 2>/dev/null || echo "$story_id")
    local role
    role=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id==$id) | .agentRole // ""' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    local update_script="$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh"
    [ ! -x "$update_script" ] && return 0
    case "$event" in
        start)
            "$update_script" story_start "$story_id" "$lane" "$role" "$title" 2>/dev/null || true
            ;;
        complete)
            "$update_script" story_complete "$story_id" "$lane" "$title" 2>/dev/null || true
            ;;
        fail)
            "$update_script" story_fail "$story_id" "$lane" "$message" 2>/dev/null || true
            ;;
    esac
}

# Invoke Claude CLI to implement a story
implement_story() {
    local story_id=$1
    local retry_count=0
    local output_file="$CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR/${story_id}_$(date +'%Y%m%d_%H%M%S').log"
    local story_started_at=$(date -Iseconds)

    local title=$(get_story_title "$story_id")
    log "Implementing story: $story_id - $title"
    update_monitor_status "start" "$story_id"

    # Check dependencies first
    if ! are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
        local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id" | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
        error "Cannot implement $story_id - dependencies not satisfied: $deps"
        return 1
    fi

    # Plan mode check: run planning agent before implementation if complexity thresholds met
    if check_plan_mode_required "$story_id"; then
        log "Plan mode required for $story_id (estimatedHours>=6, deps>=2, or flag set)"
        run_plan_mode "$story_id"
    fi

    # Resolve effort -> model + max-turns for this story (stable across retries)
    resolve_effort_settings "$story_id"
    local model_flag=()
    local turns_flag=()
    [ -n "${STORY_MODEL:-}" ]     && model_flag=(--model "$STORY_MODEL")
    [ -n "${STORY_MAX_TURNS:-}" ] && turns_flag=(--max-turns "$STORY_MAX_TURNS")

    # Resolve aiProvider -> which CLI binary to use
    resolve_provider_settings "$story_id"
    local story_cli
    story_cli=$(provider_to_cli "${STORY_PROVIDER:-claude-sonnet}")

    while [ $retry_count -le $MAX_RETRIES ]; do
        # Rebuild prompt each attempt: retry_count and KB ID must reflect current state
        local next_kb_id
        next_kb_id=$(get_next_kb_id)
        local prompt
        prompt="$(build_implementation_prompt "$story_id")
$(build_kb_prompt_section "$story_id" "$retry_count" "$next_kb_id")"

        # Log the prompt
        echo "=== Prompt for $story_id (attempt $((retry_count + 1))) ===" >> "$output_file"
        echo "$prompt" >> "$output_file"

This file tracks implementation history and patterns discovered during autonomous development.

---

EOF
    fi

    cat >> "$AGENTS_FILE" << EOF
## $story_id: $title
- **Date**: $(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
- **Phase**: ${phase:-unassigned}
- **Status**: $status
- **Log**: logs/claude_outputs/${story_id}_*.log

EOF
}

# Increment iteration counter
increment_iteration() {
    local current=$(jq -r '.currentIteration' "$PRD_FILE")
    local next=$((current + 1))
    jq ".currentIteration = $next" "$PRD_FILE" > "$PRD_FILE.tmp" && mv "$PRD_FILE.tmp" "$PRD_FILE"
}

# Show PRD status with phase information
show_status() {
    echo ""
    echo -e "${MAGENTA}=== PRD Status ===${NC}"
    echo ""

    local total=$(jq '.stories | length' "$PRD_FILE")
    local completed=$(jq '[.stories[] | select(.completed == true)] | length' "$PRD_FILE")
    local pending=$((total - completed))

    echo -e "Project: ${CYAN}$(jq -r '.project.name' "$PRD_FILE")${NC}"
    echo -e "Total Stories: $total"
    echo -e "Completed: ${GREEN}$completed${NC}"
    echo -e "Pending: ${YELLOW}$pending${NC}"
    echo ""

    # Show next recommended story
    local next=$(get_next_story)
    if [ -n "$next" ]; then
        echo -e "Next recommended: ${WHITE}$next${NC} - $(get_story_title "$next")"
        local phase=$(get_story_phase "$next")
        [ -n "$phase" ] && echo -e "                 Phase: ${CYAN}$phase${NC}"
    fi
    echo ""

    echo -e "${CYAN}Stories by Phase:${NC}"

    local phases=$(get_phases)
    if [ -n "$phases" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r phase; do
            [ -z "$phase" ] && continue
            echo -e "\n  ${WHITE}$phase:${NC}"

            local stories=$(get_phase_stories "$phase")
            while IFS= read -r story_id; do
                [ -z "$story_id" ] && continue
                local title=$(get_story_title "$story_id")
                local priority=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .priority // "medium"' "$PRD_FILE")
                local priority_badge=""
                case $priority in
                    high) priority_badge=" ${RED}[H]${NC}" ;;
                    low) priority_badge=" ${BLUE}[L]${NC}" ;;
                esac

                if is_story_completed "$story_id"; then
                    echo -e "    ${GREEN}+${NC} $story_id: $title$priority_badge"
                elif are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
                    echo -e "    ${YELLOW}o${NC} $story_id: $title$priority_badge ${CYAN}(ready)${NC}"
                else
                    local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id" | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
                    echo -e "    ${RED}x${NC} $story_id: $title$priority_badge ${RED}(blocked: $deps)${NC}"
                fi
            done <<< "$stories"
        done <<< "$phases"
    else
        # No phases, show flat list
        jq -r '.stories[] | "\(.id): \(.title) [\(if .completed then "DONE" else "PENDING" end)]"' "$PRD_FILE" | while read line; do
            if [[ "$line" == *"[DONE]"* ]]; then
                echo -e "  ${GREEN}+${NC} $line"
            else
                echo -e "  ${YELLOW}o${NC} $line"
            fi

if [ "$invoke_success" = true ]; then
            # Extract human-readable result text and append to output log
            if [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
                jq -r '.result // empty' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || cat "$json_result_file" >> "$output_file"
            fi
            echo "" >> "$output_file"
            echo "=== End $story_cli Output ===" >> "$output_file"
            success "$story_cli completed implementation for $story_id"
            update_monitor_status "complete" "$story_id" "Implementation succeeded"
            append_cost_record "$story_id" "completed" "$story_started_at" "$(date -Iseconds)" "$output_file" "$json_result_file"
            post_completion_message "$story_id" "completed"
            return 0
        else
            local exit_code=$?
            # Still capture any partial JSON output
            if [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
                jq -r '.result // empty' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || cat "$json_result_file" >> "$output_file"
            fi
            echo "" >> "$output_file"
            echo "=== $story_cli exited with code $exit_code ===" >> "$output_file"

            retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
            if [ $retry_count -le $MAX_RETRIES ]; then
                warning "$story_cli failed, retrying in ${RETRY_DELAY}s..."
                sleep $RETRY_DELAY
            fi
        fi
    done

    error "Failed to implement $story_id after $((MAX_RETRIES + 1)) attempts"
    update_monitor_status "fail" "$story_id" "Failed after $((MAX_RETRIES + 1)) attempts"
    append_cost_record "$story_id" "failed" "$story_started_at" "$(date -Iseconds)" "$output_file" "$json_result_file"
    post_completion_message "$story_id" "failed"
    return 1
}

# Update story status in PRD
update_story_status() {
    local story_id=$1
    local status=$2  # "completed" or "failed"
    local timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)
    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
    local lock_file="${prd_target}.lock"

    local tmp_file="${prd_target}.tmp.$$"

    (
        flock -w 10 200 || { error "Could not acquire lock on $prd_target"; return 1; }

        if [ "$status" = "completed" ]; then
            jq --arg id "$story_id" --arg ts "$timestamp" \
                '(.stories[] | select(.id == $id)) |= . + {completed: true, status: "completed", completedAt: $ts}' \
                "$prd_target" > "$tmp_file" && mv "$tmp_file" "$prd_target"
            success "Story $story_id marked as completed"
            update_agents_file "$story_id" "completed"
        else
            jq --arg id "$story_id" --arg ts "$timestamp" \
                '(.stories[] | select(.id == $id)) |= . + {status: "failed", lastAttempt: $ts}' \
                "$prd_target" > "$tmp_file" && mv "$tmp_file" "$prd_target"
            warning "Story $story_id marked as failed"

warning "Story $story_id marked as failed"
            update_agents_file "$story_id" "failed"
        fi
        # Sync PRD_FILE to orchestrations/game-prd.json if it's a non-default PRD (for dashboard viewers)
        local default_prd="$AUTOMATION_DIR/prd.json"
        if [ "$prd_target" != "$default_prd" ] && [ -f "$prd_target" ]; then
            local sync_dest="$AUTOMATION_DIR/game-prd.json"
            cp "$prd_target" "$sync_dest" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    ) 200>"$lock_file"
}

# Append a cost/time record to phase-cost.jsonl
# Called after each story completes (success or failure) for phase-aware tracking
append_cost_record() {
    local story_id=$1 status=$2 started_at=$3 ended_at=$4 output_file=$5 json_result_file=${6:-}
    local cost_file="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs/phase-cost.jsonl"
    local lock_file="${cost_file}.lock"

    # Read story metadata from prd.json
    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
    local title=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .title // "unknown"' "$prd_target")
    local agent_id=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // "unknown"' "$prd_target")
    local forecast_hours=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .estimatedHours // 0' "$prd_target")
    local phase_id="${CURRENT_PHASE:-}"
    if [ -z "$phase_id" ]; then
        # Look up phase from implementationOrder when not set by --phase flag
        phase_id=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
            '.implementationOrder | to_entries[] | select(.value | contains([$id])) | .key' \
            "$prd_target" | head -1)
        [ -z "$phase_id" ] && phase_id="unknown"
    fi

    # Compute elapsed minutes
    local start_epoch=$(date -d "$started_at" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    local end_epoch=$(date -d "$ended_at" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    local elapsed_minutes=0
    if [ "$start_epoch" -gt 0 ] && [ "$end_epoch" -gt 0 ]; then
        elapsed_minutes=$(echo "scale=2; ($end_epoch - $start_epoch) / 60" | bc 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    fi

    # Parse cost/token/turn usage from Claude CLI JSON result (--output-format json)
    local tokens_in=0 tokens_out=0 cost_usd=0 task_turns=0
    if [ -n "$json_result_file" ] && [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
        cost_usd=$(jq -r '.total_cost_usd // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
        tokens_in=$(jq -r '.usage.input_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
        tokens_out=$(jq -r '.usage.output_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
        # Turn count: Claude CLI may report num_turns or turns
        task_turns=$(jq -r '.num_turns // .turns // .usage.turns // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
        # Also capture cache tokens if present
        local cache_create=$(jq -r '.usage.cache_creation_input_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
        local cache_read=$(jq -r '.usage.cache_read_input_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
        # Total input includes base + cache tokens
        tokens_in=$(( ${tokens_in:-0} + ${cache_create:-0} + ${cache_read:-0} ))
    fi
    [ -z "$tokens_in" ] && tokens_in=0
    [ -z "$tokens_out" ] && tokens_out=0
    [ -z "$cost_usd" ] && cost_usd=0
    [ -z "$task_turns" ] && task_turns=0

    # Idempotency guard: skip if this story already has a record with same status
    if grep -q "\"story_id\":\"${story_id}\".*\"status\":\"${status}\"" "$cost_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        warning "Cost record for $story_id ($status) already exists — skipping duplicate"
        return 0
    fi

# Atomic JSONL append with flock
    (
        flock -w 10 200 || { error "Could not acquire lock on $cost_file"; return 1; }
        jq -cn \
            --arg pid "$phase_id" --arg pn "$phase_id" \
            --arg sid "$story_id" --arg st "$title" \
            --arg aid "$agent_id" --arg an "$agent_id" \
            --argjson fh "${forecast_hours:-0}" --argjson fc 0 \
            --arg sa "$started_at" --arg ea "$ended_at" \
            --argjson em "${elapsed_minutes:-0}" --argjson cu "$cost_usd" \
            --argjson ti "${tokens_in:-0}" --argjson to "${tokens_out:-0}" \
            --argjson tt "${task_turns:-0}" \
            --argjson cr "${cache_read:-0}" --argjson cc "${cache_create:-0}" \
            --arg s "$status" --arg n "" \
            '{phase_id:$pid, phase_name:$pn, story_id:$sid, story_title:$st,
              agent_id:$aid, agent_name:$an, forecast_hours:$fh, forecast_cost_usd:$fc,
              started_at:$sa, ended_at:$ea, elapsed_minutes:$em,
              task_cost_usd:$cu, task_tokens_in:$ti, task_tokens_out:$to,
              task_turns:$tt, cache_read_tokens:$cr, cache_create_tokens:$cc,
              status:$s, notes:$n}' >> "$cost_file"
    ) 200>"$lock_file"
}

# Return the next sequential KB entry ID (KB-001, KB-002, ...) by reading orchestrations/agents/KB.md
get_next_kb_id() {
    local kb_file="$AUTOMATION_DIR/agents/KB.md"
    if [ ! -f "$kb_file" ]; then
        echo "KB-001"
        return
    fi
    local last_num
    last_num=$(grep -oP '(?<=^## KB-)\d+' "$kb_file" | sort -n | tail -1)
    if [ -z "$last_num" ]; then
        echo "KB-001"
    else
        printf "KB-%03d" $((last_num + 1))
    fi
}

# Return KB entries from orchestrations/agents/KB.md whose AgentRole matches the current story

item.text: I’ve got runtime resolution flowing through execution. The remaining `claude.sh` work is the control surface: a hidden `--validate-phase` entry point for preflight checks and a hidden `--self-test` entry point so the precedence/validation/logging cases can be verified without adding files outside scope.

I’m editing `estimate-stories.sh` next. The goal there is not to change the whole estimator, just to make model resolution first-class: validate it with the same precedence, use the resolved model in the output, and carry it through the JSON result payload that downstream dashboards consume.

I’m wiring the orchestration wrapper to use `claude.sh` as the metadata validator before any phase work starts. After that, the same resolved provider/model is shown in the printed execution plan and embedded in the initial monitor payload so the dashboards get stable, phase-wide values from the start.

I noticed one field-name mismatch while threading the data through: the monitor payload was using `resolvedProvider`, but the PRD persistence hook was writing `resolvedAiProvider`. I’m aligning that now so the dashboards see the same field names regardless of source.

The code changes are in place. I’m running bash syntax checks first, then the hidden self-test harness in `claude.sh`, and then a dry-run of the estimator/orchestrator so I can catch output-shape issues before finishing.

item.text: The first validation pass caught something useful: the new phase default was leaking onto legacy `codex` stories in `mvp_cli_control` during orchestration output. I’m fixing that so phase defaults only apply to `epam` stories; legacy providers keep their own compatibility path.
**Adding run-agent helper and story map condition**

The EPAM-only default leak is fixed. I’m rerunning syntax plus the same validation commands to confirm the phase now shows legacy `codex` stories with legacy-effort models and the EPAM stories with phase/story-resolved models.

+      "description": "Implement a ContextGuard module (sibling to BudgetGuard) that tracks cumulative tokens consumed by file-reading tools (ReadFile, Search, ListFiles, Bash output) during an agent session. When the running total approaches a configurable ceiling, warn the agent to narrow scope; when exceeded, block further file-read tool calls and inject a budget-exhausted message. Prevents runaway context loading where the agent reads the entire project for a narrow-scope task (observed: 974K cache_read_tokens for a single-file Python script in a TypeScript project).  # Orchestration mode — inherited from run-agent-orchestration.sh or set directly

-# Effort -> model + max-turns mapping
-# Stories carry an optional "effort" field: low | medium (default) | high

     local effort

+

+    log "  Effort[$effort] files=$file_count -> turns=${STORY_MAX_TURNS:-unlimited}"
+}
+
+# legacy_model_for_effort <effort>
+legacy_model_for_effort() {
+    case "$1" in

+        high) echo "$EFFORT_MODEL_HIGH" ;;
+        *) echo "$EFFORT_MODEL_MEDIUM" ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+provider_is_supported() {
+    case "$1" in

+        *) return 1 ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+get_story_runtime_json() {
+    local story_id="$1"
+    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
+    local phase_id="${2:-$(get_story_phase "$story_id")}"
+
+    jq -c --arg id "$story_id" --arg phase "${phase_id:-unknown}" '
+        (.configuration.aiRuntime // {}) as $runtime |
+        (.phasesConfig[$phase] // {}) as $phase_cfg |
+        (.stories[] | select(.id == $id)) as $story |
+        {
+            storyId: $story.id,
+            phaseId: (if ($phase | length) > 0 then $phase else "unknown" end),
+            provider: ($story.aiProvider // $phase_cfg.aiProvider // $runtime.provider // "epam"),
+            storyModel: ($story.model // ""),
+            phaseDefaultModel: ($phase_cfg.defaultModel // ""),
+            projectDefaultModel: ($runtime.defaultModel // ""),
+            allowedModels: ($runtime.allowedModels // []),
+            effort: ($story.effort // "medium")
+        }' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null
+}
+
+resolve_story_runtime_settings() {
+    local story_id="$1"
+    local phase_id="${2:-$(get_story_phase "$story_id")}"
+    local runtime_json
+    runtime_json=$(get_story_runtime_json "$story_id" "$phase_id")
+
+    if [ -z "$runtime_json" ] || [ "$runtime_json" = "null" ]; then
+        error "Runtime resolution failed for $story_id: story not found in $(basename "${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}")"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    STORY_PHASE=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.phaseId')
+    STORY_PROVIDER=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.provider')
+    STORY_PHASE_DEFAULT_MODEL=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.phaseDefaultModel')
+    STORY_PROJECT_DEFAULT_MODEL=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.projectDefaultModel')
+
+    if ! provider_is_supported "$STORY_PROVIDER"; then
+        error "Invalid aiProvider for $story_id: '$STORY_PROVIDER'. Supported values: epam, codex, opencode, codemie-claude, claude*"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    local story_model phase_model project_model allowed_models
+    story_model=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.storyModel')
+    phase_model=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.phaseDefaultModel')
+    project_model=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.projectDefaultModel')
+    allowed_models=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.allowedModels[]?' 2>/dev/null || true)
+
+    STORY_MODEL=""
+    STORY_MODEL_SOURCE=""
+
+    if [ -n "$story_model" ]; then
+        STORY_MODEL="$story_model"
+        STORY_MODEL_SOURCE="story"
+    elif [ "$STORY_PROVIDER" = "epam" ] && [ -n "$phase_model" ]; then
+        STORY_MODEL="$phase_model"
+        STORY_MODEL_SOURCE="phase"
+    elif [ "$STORY_PROVIDER" = "epam" ] && [ -n "$project_model" ]; then
+        STORY_MODEL="$project_model"
+        STORY_MODEL_SOURCE="project"
+    elif [ "$STORY_PROVIDER" != "epam" ]; then
+        STORY_MODEL=$(legacy_model_for_effort "$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.effort')")
+        STORY_MODEL_SOURCE="legacy-effort"
+    fi
+
+    if [ "$STORY_PROVIDER" = "epam" ]; then
+        if [ -z "$STORY_MODEL" ]; then
+            error "Missing model for $story_id. Resolution order is story.model -> phasesConfig[${STORY_PHASE}].defaultModel -> configuration.aiRuntime.defaultModel."
+            return 1
+        fi
+        if ! echo "$allowed_models" | grep -Fxq "$STORY_MODEL"; then
+            error "Invalid model for $story_id: '$STORY_MODEL'. Allowed models: $(echo "$allowed_models" | paste -sd ', ' -)"
+            return 1
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    log "  Runtime[$story_id] provider=$STORY_PROVIDER model=${STORY_MODEL:-n/a} source=${STORY_MODEL_SOURCE:-none} cli=$(provider_to_cli "$STORY_PROVIDER")"
 }

+                    var_cost: (if length > 1 then

+                      else 0 end)
+                }
+            }) | from_entries')
+

+        jq --argjson obs "$category_data" \
+           --arg ts "$(date -Iseconds)" \
+           '.updatedAt = $ts |
+            .categories = (.categories // {} |
+              reduce ($obs | to_entries[]) as $e (.;
+                .[$e.key] = {
+                    n: ((.[$e.key].n // 0) + $e.value.n),
+                    mean_minutes: $e.value.mean_min,
+                    mean_cost: $e.value.mean_cost,
+                    mean_tokens: $e.value.mean_tok,
+                    mean_turns: $e.value.mean_turns,
+                    var_minutes: $e.value.var_min,
+                    var_cost: $e.value.var_cost,
+                    updatedAt: $ts
+                }))' \
+           "$CALIBRATION_FILE" > "${CALIBRATION_FILE}.tmp" \
+           && mv "${CALIBRATION_FILE}.tmp" "$CALIBRATION_FILE"
+
+        success "Bayesian posteriors updated in $CALIBRATION_FILE"

item.text: I found one non-obvious compatibility edge while verifying: phase-level defaults must not spill onto legacy non-EPAM stories, or the orchestration UI misreports their models. That’s worth recording once in the KB since the next story on top of this work is model-visibility across dashboards.

item.aggregated_output:
- **Test framework**: vitest
- **Build tool**: tsup
- **Linter**: eslint + prettier

## Key Paths

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/index.ts` | CLI entry point |
| `src/cli/` | Commander commands, REPL, slash commands |
| `src/providers/` | LLM provider adapters (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini) |
| `src/agent/` | ReAct agent loop |
| `src/tools/` | Built-in tools (ReadFile, WriteFile, Bash, Search, etc.) |
| `src/auth/` | OAuth device flow, token management |
| `src/config/` | Config resolver (global → project → env → flags) |
| `src/billing/` | Tier detection, BYOK key store, provider selection |
| `src/context/` | Session store, context loader, memory compressor |
| `test/` | Unit and integration tests |

## Conventions

- All source in `src/`, tests in `test/unit/` mirroring src structure
- Types defined in `types.ts` per module directory
- Errors extend `EpamError` from `src/utils/errors.ts`
- Config hierarchy: CLI flags > EPAM_* env vars > .epam/settings.json > ~/.epam/config.json > defaults
- Provider chain: up to 5 LLM slots with automatic failover (circuit breaker pattern)

## Test Gate

```bash
# Must pass before phase gate approval
npx vitest run          # unit + integration tests
npx tsc --noEmit        # TypeScript strict check
```

## Auth Model

- **Free tier**: BYOK (bring your own key) — direct provider calls
- **Pro/Enterprise**: Proxy through backend-stub — JWT claims carry tier info
- **Device flow** (RFC 8628) is default auth; browser PKCE optional with `--browser`

items.text: Add PRD runtime model metadata and defaults in orchestrations/prd.json
Implement runtime provider/model resolution and validation in claude.sh
Update estimate/orchestration outputs and monitor payloads with resolved provider/model
Add and run scoped shell self-tests for precedence, validation, and cost-log payloads

    50	# resolve_turn_budget <story_id>
    51	# Keeps effort-driven turn caps, but no longer chooses the runtime model.
    52	resolve_turn_budget() {
    53	    local story_id="$1"
    54	    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
    55	    local effort
    56	    effort=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
    57	        '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .effort // "medium"' \
    58	        "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "medium")
    59	    STORY_EFFORT="$effort"
    60	
    61	    local effort_max_turns
    62	    case "$effort" in
    63	        low)
    64	            effort_max_turns=10
    65	            ;;
    66	        high)
    67	            effort_max_turns=0  # 0 = unlimited
    68	            ;;
    69	        *)  # medium (default)
    70	            effort_max_turns=30
    71	            ;;
    72	    esac
    73	
    74	    # Scale turns by file count — narrow-scope stories get a tighter budget
    75	    local file_count
    76	    file_count=$(jq --arg id "$story_id" \
    77	        '[.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .technicalNotes.files // [] | .[]] | length' \
    78	        "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    79	
    80	    if [ "$file_count" -gt 0 ] && [ "$effort_max_turns" -gt 0 ]; then
    81	        local scope_max=$(( file_count * 8 + 5 ))
    82	        if [ "$scope_max" -lt "$effort_max_turns" ]; then
    83	            STORY_MAX_TURNS=$scope_max
    84	        else
    85	            STORY_MAX_TURNS=$effort_max_turns
    86	        fi
    87	    else
    88	        STORY_MAX_TURNS=$effort_max_turns
    89	    fi
    90	
    91	    # 0 means unlimited — clear the variable so no --max-turns flag is passed
    92	    [ "${STORY_MAX_TURNS:-0}" -eq 0 ] 2>/dev/null && STORY_MAX_TURNS=""
    93	
    94	    log "  Effort[$effort] files=$file_count -> turns=${STORY_MAX_TURNS:-unlimited}"
    95	}

    97	# legacy_model_for_effort <effort>
    98	legacy_model_for_effort() {
    99	    case "$1" in
   100	        low) echo "$EFFORT_MODEL_LOW" ;;
   101	        high) echo "$EFFORT_MODEL_HIGH" ;;
   102	        *) echo "$EFFORT_MODEL_MEDIUM" ;;
   103	    esac
   104	}

   109	        *) return 1 ;;
   110	    esac

   113	get_story_runtime_json() {
   114	    local story_id="$1"
   115	    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
   116	    local phase_id="${2:-$(get_story_phase "$story_id")}"
   117	
   118	    jq -c --arg id "$story_id" --arg phase "${phase_id:-unknown}" '
   119	        (.configuration.aiRuntime // {}) as $runtime |
   120	        (.phasesConfig[$phase] // {}) as $phase_cfg |
   121	        (.stories[] | select(.id == $id)) as $story |
   122	        {
   123	            storyId: $story.id,
   124	            phaseId: (if ($phase | length) > 0 then $phase else "unknown" end),
   125	            provider: ($story.aiProvider // $phase_cfg.aiProvider // $runtime.provider // "epam"),
   126	            storyModel: ($story.model // ""),
   127	            phaseDefaultModel: ($phase_cfg.defaultModel // ""),
   128	            projectDefaultModel: ($runtime.defaultModel // ""),
   129	            allowedModels: ($runtime.allowedModels // []),
   130	            effort: ($story.effort // "medium")
   131	        }' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null
   132	}
   133	
   134	resolve_story_runtime_settings() {
   135	    local story_id="$1"
   136	    local phase_id="${2:-$(get_story_phase "$story_id")}"
   137	    local runtime_json
   138	    runtime_json=$(get_story_runtime_json "$story_id" "$phase_id")
   139	
   140	    if [ -z "$runtime_json" ] || [ "$runtime_json" = "null" ]; then
   141	        error "Runtime resolution failed for $story_id: story not found in $(basename "${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}")"
   142	        return 1
   143	    fi
   144	
   145	    STORY_PHASE=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.phaseId')
   146	    STORY_PROVIDER=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.provider')
   147	    STORY_PHASE_DEFAULT_MODEL=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.phaseDefaultModel')
   148	    STORY_PROJECT_DEFAULT_MODEL=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.projectDefaultModel')
   149	
   150	    if ! provider_is_supported "$STORY_PROVIDER"; then
   151	        error "Invalid aiProvider for $story_id: '$STORY_PROVIDER'. Supported values: epam, codex, opencode, codemie-claude, claude*"
   152	        return 1
   153	    fi
   154	
   155	    local story_model phase_model project_model allowed_models
   156	    story_model=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.storyModel')
   157	    phase_model=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.phaseDefaultModel')
   158	    project_model=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.projectDefaultModel')
   159	    allowed_models=$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.allowedModels[]?' 2>/dev/null || true)
   160	
   161	    STORY_MODEL=""
   162	    STORY_MODEL_SOURCE=""
   163	
   164	    if [ -n "$story_model" ]; then
   165	        STORY_MODEL="$story_model"
   166	        STORY_MODEL_SOURCE="story"
   167	    elif [ "$STORY_PROVIDER" = "epam" ] && [ -n "$phase_model" ]; then
   168	        STORY_MODEL="$phase_model"
   169	        STORY_MODEL_SOURCE="phase"
   170	    elif [ "$STORY_PROVIDER" = "epam" ] && [ -n "$project_model" ]; then
   171	        STORY_MODEL="$project_model"
   172	        STORY_MODEL_SOURCE="project"
   173	    elif [ "$STORY_PROVIDER" != "epam" ]; then
   174	        STORY_MODEL=$(legacy_model_for_effort "$(echo "$runtime_json" | jq -r '.effort')")
   175	        STORY_MODEL_SOURCE="legacy-effort"
   176	    fi
   177	
   178	    if [ "$STORY_PROVIDER" = "epam" ]; then
   179	        if [ -z "$STORY_MODEL" ]; then
   180	            error "Missing model for $story_id. Resolution order is story.model -> phasesConfig[${STORY_PHASE}].defaultModel -> configuration.aiRuntime.defaultModel."
   181	            return 1
   182	        fi
   183	        if ! echo "$allowed_models" | grep -Fxq "$STORY_MODEL"; then
   184	            error "Invalid model for $story_id: '$STORY_MODEL'. Allowed models: $(echo "$allowed_models" | paste -sd ', ' -)"
   185	            return 1
   186	        fi
   187	    fi
   188	
   189	    log "  Runtime[$story_id] provider=$STORY_PROVIDER model=${STORY_MODEL:-n/a} source=${STORY_MODEL_SOURCE:-none} cli=$(provider_to_cli "$STORY_PROVIDER")"
   190	}
   191	
   192	persist_story_runtime_metadata() {
   193	    local story_id="$1"
   194	    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
   195	    local lock_file="${prd_target}.lock"
   196	    local tmp_file="${prd_target}.runtime.$$"
   197	
   198	    [ -z "${STORY_PROVIDER:-}" ] && return 1
   199	
   200	    (
   201	        flock -w 10 200 || { error "Could not acquire lock on $prd_target"; return 1; }
   202	        jq --arg id "$story_id" \
   203	           --arg provider "${STORY_PROVIDER:-}" \
   204	           --arg model "${STORY_MODEL:-}" \
   205	           --arg source "${STORY_MODEL_SOURCE:-}" \
   206	           '(.stories[] | select(.id == $id)) |=
   207	             . + {
   208	               resolvedProvider: $provider,
   209	               resolvedModel: $model,
   210	               resolvedModelSource: $source
   211	             }' \
   212	           "$prd_target" > "$tmp_file" && mv "$tmp_file" "$prd_target"
   213	    ) 200>"$lock_file"
   214	}

   216	validate_story_runtime_settings() {
   217	    local story_id="$1"
   218	    local phase_id="${2:-$(get_story_phase "$story_id")}"
   219	    resolve_turn_budget "$story_id"
   220	    resolve_story_runtime_settings "$story_id" "$phase_id"

   223	# provider_to_cli <provider>
   224	# Returns the CLI binary name for a given aiProvider value.
   225	provider_to_cli() {
   226	    case "$1" in
   227	        opencode)       echo "opencode" ;;
   228	        codex)          echo "codex" ;;
   229	        codemie-claude) echo "codemie-claude" ;;
   230	        epam)           echo "$CLAUDE_CMD" ;;  # epam: treat same as claude for now

900	            ;;
   901	    esac
   902	}

   904	# Invoke Claude CLI to implement a story
   905	implement_story() {
   906	    local story_id=$1
   907	    local retry_count=0
   908	    local output_file="$CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR/${story_id}_$(date +'%Y%m%d_%H%M%S').log"
   909	    local story_started_at=$(date -Iseconds)
   910	
   911	    local title=$(get_story_title "$story_id")
   912	    log "Implementing story: $story_id - $title"
   913	    update_monitor_status "start" "$story_id"
   914	
   915	    # Check dependencies first
   916	    if ! are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
   917	        local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id" | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
   918	        error "Cannot implement $story_id - dependencies not satisfied: $deps"
   919	        return 1
   920	    fi
   921	
   922	    # Plan mode check: run planning agent before implementation if complexity thresholds met
   923	    if check_plan_mode_required "$story_id"; then
   924	        log "Plan mode required for $story_id (estimatedHours>=6, deps>=2, or flag set)"
   925	        run_plan_mode "$story_id"
   926	    fi
   927	
   928	    if ! validate_story_runtime_settings "$story_id"; then
   929	        error "Cannot implement $story_id - invalid runtime provider/model selection"
   930	        return 1
   931	    fi
   932	    persist_story_runtime_metadata "$story_id" || true
   933	
   934	    local model_flag=()
   935	    local turns_flag=()
   936	    [ -n "${STORY_MODEL:-}" ]     && model_flag=(--model "$STORY_MODEL")
   937	    [ -n "${STORY_MAX_TURNS:-}" ] && turns_flag=(--max-turns "$STORY_MAX_TURNS")
   938	    local story_cli
   939	    story_cli=$(provider_to_cli "${STORY_PROVIDER:-epam}")
   940	
   941	    while [ $retry_count -le $MAX_RETRIES ]; do
   942	        # Rebuild prompt each attempt: retry_count and KB ID must reflect current state
   943	        local next_kb_id
   944	        next_kb_id=$(get_next_kb_id)
   945	        local prompt
   946	        prompt="$(build_implementation_prompt "$story_id")
   947	$(build_kb_prompt_section "$story_id" "$retry_count" "$next_kb_id")"
   948	
   949	        # Log the prompt
   950	        echo "=== Prompt for $story_id (attempt $((retry_count + 1))) ===" >> "$output_file"
   951	        echo "$prompt" >> "$output_file"
   952	        echo "=== End Prompt ===" >> "$output_file"
   953	        echo "" >> "$output_file"
   954	
   955	        log "Invoking $story_cli (attempt $((retry_count + 1))/$((MAX_RETRIES + 1)))..."
   956	
   957	        # Change to project root for the CLI to have correct context
   958	        cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
   959	
   960	        echo "=== $story_cli Output (attempt $((retry_count + 1))) ===" >> "$output_file"
   961	
   962	        local json_result_file="${output_file%.log}_result.json"
   963	        local invoke_success=false
   964	
   965	        case "${STORY_PROVIDER:-epam}" in
   966	            opencode)
   967	                # OpenCode: pass prompt via temp file (prompts can exceed arg limits)
   968	                # --format json emits JSONL stream; we normalize it after
   969	                local raw_file="${json_result_file%.json}_raw.jsonl"
   970	                local prompt_file="${json_result_file%.json}_prompt.txt"
   971	                echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"
   972	                if opencode run --format json "$(cat "$prompt_file")" \
   973	                        > "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null; then
   974	                    normalize_provider_json "opencode" "$raw_file" "$json_result_file"
   975	                    # Append text output to log
   976	                    grep '"type":"text"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
   977	                        | jq -r '.part.text // .text // empty' 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || true
   978	                    invoke_success=true
   979	                fi
   980	                rm -f "$prompt_file"
   981	                ;;
   982	            codex)
   983	                # Codex: reads prompt from stdin when '-' is passed
   984	                # --json emits JSONL stream; we normalize it after
   985	                local raw_file="${json_result_file%.json}_raw.jsonl"
   986	                if echo "$prompt" | codex exec --json - \
   987	                        > "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null; then
   988	                    normalize_provider_json "codex" "$raw_file" "$json_result_file"
   989	                    # Append text output to log
   990	                    grep '"type":"item.completed"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
   991	                        | jq -r '.item.text // empty' 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || true
   992	                    invoke_success=true
   993	                fi
   994	                ;;
   995	            codemie-claude)
   996	                # codemie-claude: same invocation pattern as claude — --print --output-format json
   997	                if echo "$prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE codemie-claude --print --output-format json \
   998	                        "${model_flag[@]}" "${turns_flag[@]}" "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" \
   999	                        2>/dev/null > "$json_result_file"; then
  1000	                    invoke_success=true
  1001	                fi
  1002	                ;;
  1003	            epam)
  1004	                # epam: treat same as claude — same CLI, same output format
  1005	                if echo "$prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --print --output-format json \
  1006	                        "${model_flag[@]}" "${turns_flag[@]}" "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" \
  1007	                        2>/dev/null > "$json_result_file"; then
  1008	                    invoke_success=true
  1009	                fi
  1010	                ;;
  1011	            *)
  1012	                # Claude (claude-sonnet, claude-opus, or any claude-* value)
  1013	                # --print --output-format json captures cost+tokens in a single JSON object
  1014	                if echo "$prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --print --output-format json \
  1015	                        "${model_flag[@]}" "${turns_flag[@]}" "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" \
  1016	                        2>/dev/null > "$json_result_file"; then
  1017	                    invoke_success=true
  1018	                fi
  1019	                ;;
  1020	        esac
  1021	
  1022	        if [ "$invoke_success" = true ]; then
  1023	            # Extract human-readable result text and append to output log
  1024	            if [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
  1025	                jq -r '.result // empty' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || cat "$json_result_file" >> "$output_file"
  1026	            fi
  1027	            echo "" >> "$output_file"
  1028	            echo "=== End $story_cli Output ===" >> "$output_file"
  1029	            success "$story_cli completed implementation for $story_id"
  1030	            update_monitor_status "complete" "$story_id" "Implementation succeeded"
  1031	            append_cost_record "$story_id" "completed" "$story_started_at" "$(date -Iseconds)" "$output_file" "$json_result_file"
  1032	            post_completion_message "$story_id" "completed"
  1033	            return 0
  1034	        else
  1035	            local exit_code=$?
  1036	            # Still capture any partial JSON output
  1037	            if [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
  1038	                jq -r '.result // empty' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || cat "$json_result_file" >> "$output_file"
  1039	            fi
  1040	            echo "" >> "$output_file"
  1041	            echo "=== $story_cli exited with code $exit_code ===" >> "$output_file"
  1042	
  1043	            retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
  1044	            if [ $retry_count -le $MAX_RETRIES ]; then
  1045	                warning "$story_cli failed, retrying in ${RETRY_DELAY}s..."
  1046	                sleep $RETRY_DELAY
  1047	            fi
  1048	        fi
  1049	    done
  1050	
  1051	    error "Failed to implement $story_id after $((MAX_RETRIES + 1)) attempts"
  1052	    update_monitor_status "fail" "$story_id" "Failed after $((MAX_RETRIES + 1)) attempts"
  1053	    append_cost_record "$story_id" "failed" "$story_started_at" "$(date -Iseconds)" "$output_file" "$json_result_file"
  1054	    post_completion_message "$story_id" "failed"
  1055	    return 1
  1056	}

  1058	# Update story status in PRD
  1059	update_story_status() {
  1060	    local story_id=$1
  1061	    local status=$2  # "completed" or "failed"
  1062	    local timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)
  1063	    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
  1064	    local lock_file="${prd_target}.lock"
  1065	
  1066	    local tmp_file="${prd_target}.tmp.$$"
  1067	
  1068	    (
  1069	        flock -w 10 200 || { error "Could not acquire lock on $prd_target"; return 1; }
  1070	
  1071	        if [ "$status" = "completed" ]; then
  1072	            jq --arg id "$story_id" --arg ts "$timestamp" \
  1073	                '(.stories[] | select(.id == $id)) |= . + {completed: true, status: "completed", completedAt: $ts}' \
  1074	                "$prd_target" > "$tmp_file" && mv "$tmp_file" "$prd_target"
  1075	            success "Story $story_id marked as completed"
  1076	            update_agents_file "$story_id" "completed"
  1077	        else
  1078	            jq --arg id "$story_id" --arg ts "$timestamp" \
  1079	                '(.stories[] | select(.id == $id)) |= . + {status: "failed", lastAttempt: $ts}' \
  1080	                "$prd_target" > "$tmp_file" && mv "$tmp_file" "$prd_target"
  1081	            warning "Story $story_id marked as failed"
  1082	            update_agents_file "$story_id" "failed"
  1083	        fi
  1084	        # Sync PRD_FILE to orchestrations/game-prd.json if it's a non-default PRD (for dashboard viewers)
  1085	        local default_prd="$AUTOMATION_DIR/prd.json"
  1086	        if [ "$prd_target" != "$default_prd" ] && [ -f "$prd_target" ]; then
  1087	            local sync_dest="$AUTOMATION_DIR/game-prd.json"
  1088	            cp "$prd_target" "$sync_dest" 2>/dev/null || true
  1089	        fi
  1090	    ) 200>"$lock_file"
  1091	}
  1092	
  1093	# Append a cost/time record to phase-cost.jsonl
  1094	# Called after each story completes (success or failure) for phase-aware tracking
  1095	append_cost_record() {
  1096	    local story_id=$1 status=$2 started_at=$3 ended_at=$4 output_file=$5 json_result_file=${6:-}
  1097	    local cost_file="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs/phase-cost.jsonl"
  1098	    local lock_file="${cost_file}.lock"
  1099	
  1100	    # Read story metadata from prd.json
  1101	    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
  1102	    local title=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .title // "unknown"' "$prd_target")
  1103	    local agent_id=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // "unknown"' "$prd_target")
  1104	    local forecast_hours=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .estimatedHours // 0' "$prd_target")
  1105	    local phase_id="${CURRENT_PHASE:-}"
  1106	    if [ -z "$phase_id" ]; then
  1107	        # Look up phase from implementationOrder when not set by --phase flag
  1108	        phase_id=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
  1109	            '.implementationOrder | to_entries[] | select(.value | contains([$id])) | .key' \
  1110	            "$prd_target" | head -1)
  1111	        [ -z "$phase_id" ] && phase_id="unknown"
  1112	    fi
  1113	
  1114	    # Compute elapsed minutes
  1115	    local start_epoch=$(date -d "$started_at" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  1116	    local end_epoch=$(date -d "$ended_at" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  1117	    local elapsed_minutes=0
  1118	    if [ "$start_epoch" -gt 0 ] && [ "$end_epoch" -gt 0 ]; then
  1119	        elapsed_minutes=$(echo "scale=2; ($end_epoch - $start_epoch) / 60" | bc 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  1120	    fi
  1121	
  1122	    # Parse cost/token/turn usage from Claude CLI JSON result (--output-format json)
  1123	    local tokens_in=0 tokens_out=0 cost_usd=0 task_turns=0
  1124	    if [ -n "$json_result_file" ] && [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
  1125	        cost_usd=$(jq -r '.total_cost_usd // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
  1126	        tokens_in=$(jq -r '.usage.input_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
  1127	        tokens_out=$(jq -r '.usage.output_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
  1128	        # Turn count: Claude CLI may report num_turns or turns
  1129	        task_turns=$(jq -r '.num_turns // .turns // .usage.turns // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
  1130	        # Also capture cache tokens if present
  1131	        local cache_create=$(jq -r '.usage.cache_creation_input_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
  1132	        local cache_read=$(jq -r '.usage.cache_read_input_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
  1133	        # Total input includes base + cache tokens
  1134	        tokens_in=$(( ${tokens_in:-0} + ${cache_create:-0} + ${cache_read:-0} ))
  1135	    fi
  1136	    [ -z "$tokens_in" ] && tokens_in=0
  1137	    [ -z "$tokens_out" ] && tokens_out=0
  1138	    [ -z "$cost_usd" ] && cost_usd=0
  1139	    [ -z "$task_turns" ] && task_turns=0
  1140	
  1141	    # Idempotency guard: skip if this story already has a record with same status
  1142	    if grep -q "\"story_id\":\"${story_id}\".*\"status\":\"${status}\"" "$cost_file" 2>/dev/null; then
  1143	        warning "Cost record for $story_id ($status) already exists — skipping duplicate"
  1144	        return 0
  1145	    fi
  1146	
  1147	    # Atomic JSONL append with flock
  1148	    (
  1149	        flock -w 10 200 || { error "Could not acquire lock on $cost_file"; return 1; }
  1150	        jq -cn \
  1151	            --arg pid "$phase_id" --arg pn "$phase_id" \
  1152	            --arg sid "$story_id" --arg st "$title" \
  1153	            --arg aid "$agent_id" --arg an "$agent_id" \
  1154	            --arg rp "${STORY_PROVIDER:-}" --arg rm "${STORY_MODEL:-}" \
  1155	            --argjson fh "${forecast_hours:-0}" --argjson fc 0 \
  1156	            --arg sa "$started_at" --arg ea "$ended_at" \
  1157	            --argjson em "${elapsed_minutes:-0}" --argjson cu "$cost_usd" \
  1158	            --argjson ti "${tokens_in:-0}" --argjson to "${tokens_out:-0}" \
  1159	            --argjson tt "${task_turns:-0}" \
  1160	            --argjson cr "${cache_read:-0}" --argjson cc "${cache_create:-0}" \
  1161	            --arg s "$status" --arg n "" \
  1162	            '{phase_id:$pid, phase_name:$pn, story_id:$sid, story_title:$st,
  1163	              agent_id:$aid, agent_name:$an, forecast_hours:$fh, forecast_cost_usd:$fc,
  1164	              started_at:$sa, ended_at:$ea, elapsed_minutes:$em,
  1165	              resolved_provider:$rp, resolved_model:$rm,
  1166	              task_cost_usd:$cu, task_tokens_in:$ti, task_tokens_out:$to,
  1167	              task_turns:$tt, cache_read_tokens:$cr, cache_create_tokens:$cc,
  1168	              status:$s, notes:$n}' >> "$cost_file"
  1169	    ) 200>"$lock_file"
  1170	}
